title: 10 Herbal Creams For Itchy Skin
intro_desc: Herbal creams for itchy skin can be a game-changer for people who suffer from dry, irritated skin. So, why do herbal teas help? Well, the truth is, it's not just the tea that's the magic worker, but rather the herbs themselves. When you apply a topical cream infused with these herbs, they directly interact with your skin, soothing and calming it.  For example, Calendula officinalis has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling. Urtica dioica, also known as nettle, contains compounds that can help calm the skin and reduce itching. And then there's Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, which is known for its antiseptic properties that can help combat bacterial infections that can cause itching.  Using herbal creams for itchy skin can bring many benefits to your life. Not only can they help alleviate itching and discomfort, but they can also help promote healthy skin by reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Plus, herbal creams are often natural and gentle, making them a great alternative to harsh, chemical-based products. This means you can use them on sensitive areas without worrying about irritation or other side effects.  When you choose herbal creams for itchy skin, look for products that contain high-quality, pure ingredients. Some things to consider are the concentration of the herbs, the presence of any additives or preservatives, and the manufacturing process. By taking the time to research and choose the right product, you can find a solution that really works for your itchy skin and helps you feel more comfortable and confident in your own skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe itchy skin. Calendula's triterpenoids, such as oleanolic acid and ursolic acid, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The flavonoids present in Calendula officinalis, including quercetin and kaempferol, have potent antioxidant effects that can help to calm irritated skin. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Calendula officinalis creams can provide relief from itchy skin by addressing its underlying causes.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for itchy skin
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Make Calendula infusion: Steep 1 cup of Calendula flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes. Strain and let it cool.
Mix oil and butter: Combine 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a small saucepan.
Melt and combine: Melt the oil and butter mixture over low heat. Add the cooled Calendula infusion and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil. Stir well.
Pour and set: Pour the mixture into small jars or containers. Let it cool and set at room temperature. Your Calendula cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams contains bioactive constituents like histamine, leukotrienes, and quercetin that help soothe itchy skin. These compounds work by reducing inflammation and blocking the production of chemical mediators that cause itching. Histamine and leukotrienes, naturally present in Urtica dioica, have anti-inflammatory properties that help calm irritated skin. Quercetin, a powerful antioxidant, also contributes to the reduction of inflammation and itching. When applied topically, the bioactive constituents in Urtica dioica creams can help alleviate itchy skin by addressing the underlying causes of inflammation and itching.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal creams for itchy skin
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Urtica dioica leaves, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Make a tea by steeping 1 cup of dried Urtica dioica leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Let it cool.
In a small saucepan, melt the coconut oil and beeswax over low heat. Stir until the beeswax is fully melted.
Add the cooled Urtica dioica tea, shea butter, and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify. Store the Urtica dioica cream in a cool place and use it on itchy skin as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains compounds like cineole, limonene, and beta-pinene. These bioactive constituents have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm itchy skin. The antiseptic properties of cineole reduce the risk of infection, while the anti-inflammatory properties of limonene and beta-pinene reduce redness and swelling. These properties work together to provide fast and effective relief from itchy skin. The skin-healing properties of these compounds help to speed up the healing process and leave skin feeling smoother and healthier.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for itchy skin
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 10 drops of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil.
Melt beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each heating.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Slowly add distilled water to the mixture, stirring constantly to avoid separation.
Once the mixture has cooled slightly, add Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil and mix well. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contain bioactive constituents like vitamins A, C, and E, as well as amino acids and fatty acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling in itchy skin. The gel also contains salicylic acid, a natural exfoliant that gently removes dead skin cells and promotes healing. The moisturizing properties of aloe vera help lock in moisture, soothing dry and itchy skin. Aloe barbadensis creams' anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties make it an effective solution for itchy skin.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for itchy skin
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of aloe vera gel, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 10 drops of lavender essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
In a separate bowl, mix the aloe vera gel and shea butter until well combined.
Slowly add the melted oil mixture to the aloe vera mixture, stirring constantly until smooth and creamy.
Add the lavender essential oil and stir well. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and thicken before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams contains the bioactive constituents thymoquinone, flavonoids, and sesquiterpene lactones. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to calm itchy skin. The flavonoids in Arnica montana creams have antioxidant properties, which reduce inflammation and promote healing of the skin. The sesquiterpene lactones have been shown to have a strong anti-inflammatory effect, which helps to reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy skin. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Arnica montana creams can help to provide relief from itchy skin.
remedies.image_alt: Arnica montana herbal creams for itchy skin
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Arnica montana flowers and 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a clean bowl.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water until it reaches 160°F (71°C).
Add the dried Arnica montana flowers to the coconut oil and let it infuse for 2-3 hours or overnight. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
In a separate bowl, mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter until they are well combined.
Add the infused Arnica montana liquid to the beeswax and shea butter mixture and stir until it forms a smooth cream. Pour the cream into a jar and let it cool before using.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams contains the bioactive constituents allantoin, rosmarinic acid, and ursolic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to calm itchy skin. Allantoin, in particular, is known to promote skin regeneration and reduce inflammation, making it an effective ingredient in treating itchy skin. Rosmarinic acid has antioxidant properties that help to protect the skin from damage and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting skin regeneration, Symphytum officinale creams can provide relief from itchy skin.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal creams for itchy skin
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Symphytum officinale leaves and flowers. Chop them finely.
Combine the chopped Symphytum officinale with 2 cups of coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat it over low heat for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter to the oil mixture. Stir until melted and well combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers. Let it cool and harden before use as a cream for itchy skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains the active constituents glycyrrhetic acid and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce redness and irritation in itchy skin. Glycyrrhetic acid also has a moisturizing effect, which helps to lock in moisture and calm dry, itchy skin. The flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and moisturizing the skin, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can provide relief from itchy skin and promote a healthy, balanced skin environment.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for itchy skin
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 10 drops of Glycyrrhiza glabra extract.
Melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly.
Add the Glycyrrhiza glabra extract to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely before applying it to itchy skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams contain a number of active constituents, including vitamins A, C, and E, as well as various minerals and amino acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help to calm and comfort itchy skin. The gel-like substance in aloe vera creams also contains a compound called aloin, which has been shown to have anti-irritant properties that can help to reduce redness and inflammation associated with itchy skin. Additionally, aloe vera creams contain hyaluronic acid, which can help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin. By applying aloe vera creams to itchy skin, the anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of these active constituents can work together to help provide relief and comfort.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal creams for itchy skin
remedy_procedure: Get 1 cup of aloe vera gel from a fresh aloe vera leaf. Scoop the gel with a spoon and transfer it to a bowl.
Add 1/4 cup of coconut oil to the bowl with the aloe vera gel. Mix well until combined.
Add 2 tablespoons of honey to the bowl and mix until smooth.
Add 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to the bowl and mix until combined.
Transfer the mixture to a container and refrigerate for 2 hours before use. Apply to itchy skin as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy skin. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Echinacea purpurea also have antioxidant effects, which can soothe and calm irritated skin. The alkaloids, such as echinacoside, can help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation. By applying Echinacea purpurea creams to itchy skin, these active constituents can work together to provide relief and comfort.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for itchy skin
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Echinacea purpurea extract, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until melted.
Add shea butter and stir until fully incorporated and melted.
Add Echinacea purpurea extract and vitamin E oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden. Once set, your Echinacea purpurea cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains linalool and linalyl acetate, two active compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties help to soothe itchy skin by reducing inflammation and preventing infection. The antiseptic properties of linalool and linalyl acetate also help to protect the skin from bacteria and other microorganisms that can irritate itchy skin. Additionally, the calming effects of these compounds can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for itchiness. By combining these properties, Lavandula angustifolia creams can provide effective relief for itchy skin.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for itchy skin
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 teaspoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia, 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Add the shea butter and stir until melted. Remove from heat.
Add the distilled water, dried Lavandula angustifolia, and vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let cool until solid. Your Lavandula angustifolia cream is ready to use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help soothe itchy skin due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Certain herbs like chamomile, calendula, and peppermint have been known to calm irritated skin and reduce itching. These teas can be consumed hot or cold, and some people find them to be a natural remedy for relieving itchiness.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas daily for itchy skin can be safe, but it's essential to choose teas with ingredients that are known to help with skin issues. Look for teas with soothing herbs like chamomile, calendula, or licorice root. Some herbal teas may interact with medications or have side effects, so be sure to check the ingredients and follow the recommended dosage.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with itchy skin, but it depends on the type of tea and how sensitive you are. Some people notice relief within a few hours or days after drinking tea like calendula or oat straw, while others might feel better after a week or two. It's different for everyone.
faq_time: For itchy skin, try drinking herbal tea in the evening. This is because many herbal teas, like chamomile and calendula, can help you relax and sleep better. When you're relaxed, your skin can heal faster. Drinking it in the evening may also help you get a good night's sleep, which is essential for skin health.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for itchy skin. This is because some herbs, like St. John's Wort, can speed up how your body processes other drugs. Other herbs, like ginkgo biloba, can slow it down. This means your medication might not work as well or could have stronger side effects.
faq_pregnant: If you're pregnant and dealing with itchy skin, some herbal teas might help soothe the itch. However, it's essential to be cautious. Some herbal teas, like pennyroyal and sage, can stimulate the uterus and cause problems. Others, like peppermint and chamomile, are generally considered safe, but it's still crucial to check their ingredients and brewing instructions carefully before consuming them.
faq_breastfeeding: If you're breastfeeding and have itchy skin, you might consider herbal teas for relief. However, be cautious with what you drink. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, are generally considered safe, but others like goldenseal and blue cohosh may not be suitable. Always check the ingredients and potential interactions.
title_2: Psoriasis Patients' 10 Favorite Herbal Creams for Itchy Skin
